summary: Iter25 focused retest of iter24 HIGH bug (PDF 500 on Cyrillic tm_name). Fix in /app/backend/routers/reimbursement.py reimbursement_pdf is correct: builds Content-Disposition with both ASCII `filename="..."` (via unicodedata.NFKD + ascii ignore) and RFC-5987 `filename*=UTF-8''<urlencoded>` (urllib.parse.quote). New retest file test_iteration_25_pdf_cyrillic_header.py directly mutates a report's tm_name to 'Демо Пенчев' in Mongo and asserts: 200, application/pdf, %PDF- magic, >1500 bytes, header contains BOTH filename= and filename*=UTF-8'', percent-decoded star form contains 'Демо Пенчев', ASCII fallback contains only <128 codepoints. Also asserts ASCII regression path. Full sweep of the requested regression suites (m1, m2, o, o3, o4, iter16, iter17, doctor_dedupe, iter24, iter25) = 59/59 GREEN.
critical_code_review_comments: reimbursement.py is now 1038 lines — the split-into-modules recommendation from iter23/iter24 still stands.
Nice: the fix uses NFKD fold + ascii ignore for the legacy filename= AND falls back to `reimbursement_{month}.pdf` if the fold produces empty string (e.g. pure-Cyrillic with no ASCII residue). Safe.
Minor: could log at debug when the ASCII fold discards characters, so operators can spot silent transliteration in server logs.
seed_data_creation: None new; retest mutates an existing report's tm_name in Mongo then restores it in the finally block.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter24 HIGH-priority Cyrillic Content-Disposition bug is FIXED and covered by a dedicated regression test at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_25_pdf_cyrillic_header.py. Header format: `attachment; filename="<asciifold>"; filename*=UTF-8''<pct-encoded>`. All requested phase O.5 suites still green.
rca of the issue: Root cause was header value passed to Starlette Response containing non-latin1 characters (tm_name). Fix at lines 788-815 of routers/reimbursement.py wraps the filename in RFC 5987 encoding (filename*=UTF-8''<quote>) and includes an ASCII-folded filename= as a legacy fallback. Reproduced 200 + correct header on Cyrillic 'Демо Пенчев'.
